_note: WHERE this skill reads + how it prices. The channel is NOT hard-coded. On the FIRST run (any of team_id / channel_id blank) the skill ASKS the user to paste the Teams channel link, resolves the IDs from it (scripts/resolve_channel.py), and persists them here so every later run reads the same place. The channel MUST be the one cowork-roi-member posts to. Prefer IDs; the link/names are only how they were resolved.
_channel_note: channel_link is the Teams channel URL the user pasted on first run; team_id + channel_id are resolved from it. If all three are blank, the skill asks for the link before reading anything.
_lookback_note: Every run reads only messages posted in the last message_lookback_days (default 15) — the latest cycle — and then keeps the latest post per person. parse_posts.py enforces this with --window-days.
_privacy_note: Never show anything at an individual level. A per-attribute (Role) breakdown renders ONLY when at least privacy_k_threshold members share that attribute; otherwise those members collapse into a single combined bar. Small, homogeneous teams (v1 scope) usually render one combined bar.
_email_note: When true, after building the dashboard the skill emails the channel members a high-level summary with the HTML dashboard and the one-page interpretation PDF attached. Recipients are the channel members (resolved at run time); no one outside the channel is added.
_team_size_note: Optional. Total expected contributors on the team. Left null in v1 (we do not compute adoption %). If set, or resolved via ListChannelMembers COUNT (never names), the dashboard can show 'N of team_size reported'. v1 shows only the count of contributors.
